nuggets.idea_one_liner: When an idol breaks, the resulting anxiety is proof that you weren't trusting God.
nuggets.why_it_hits: Exposes the 'Savior Complex' and the fragility of placing identity in a person rather than Christ.

value_equation.dream_outcome: Unshakeable peace regardless of others' actions.
value_equation.perceived_likelihood: High, anchored in the necessity of God as the only true North Star.
numerator_notes: Identifies the source of relationship anxiety as a misplaced idol.
denominator_notes: Demands the ego death of admitting pride in 'saving' someone.

nuggets.proof_quote_redacted: I do feel like my spirit is down when I'm... when I put her as my source of idolatry... And anxiety instead of peace comes in there because you recognize the vulnerability of that idol... I guess I'm trying to be like the savior. But really what I need to be focused on is continuing sprinting up the hill and find someone that's equally sprinting.
proof_anchor_lines: Anxiety comes when your idol is vulnerable.
Stop trying to save her and focus on the One who saves you.
nuggets.hook_angle_1_stakes: Your 'heartbreak' is actually an idolatry problem. Here's why you can't find peace.
nuggets.hook_angle_2_identity: Are you her Savior or are you a son of the Savior? Your anxiety tells the truth.
nuggets.hook_angle_3_mechanism: The 'Vulnerable Idol' mechanism: Why people-pleasing leads to spiritual burnout.
nuggets.enemy_frame_proof_redacted: I guess I'm trying to be like the savior
nuggets.first_step_seed: Name the person you've placed on a pedestal and ask God to take their place.
nuggets.redaction_notes: Deeply personal sharing about betrayal; treat with high respect.
must_not_claim: That the other person didn't do something wrong

nuggets.idea_one_liner: Your past sins aren't a reason to permit sin today—that's a 'regression' trap.
nuggets.why_it_hits: Destroys the 'double standard' excuse that keeps men stuck in subpar relationships.
nuggets.audience_fit: men making excuses for staying in bad dynamics

value_equation.dream_outcome: Freedom to set high standards despite a messy past.
value_equation.perceived_likelihood: High, centered on the new creation in Christ.
numerator_notes: Calls out a specific 'Satanic' logic: 'I was bad, so I have to let her be bad'.
Validates the transformation of the individual.
denominator_notes: Requires rejecting the feeling of 'unworthiness' that feels like humility but is actually pride.

nuggets.proof_quote_redacted: It also doesn't seem that she was asking for forgiveness, though... if Satan's trying to bring up, 'well, I did this, so I should allow and permit this,' then that's regression. That's making excuses for sin.
proof_anchor_lines: Using your past to justify current sin is a trap.
You're a different man now. Act like it.
nuggets.hook_angle_1_stakes: The dangerous 'Double Standard' trap that keeps good men in bad marriages.
nuggets.hook_angle_2_identity: Stop identifying with the man you used to be. You're a new creation.
nuggets.hook_angle_3_mechanism: How to set standards when you have a 'messy' track record.
nuggets.enemy_frame_proof_redacted: it feels like a double standard because I was doing the same thing
nuggets.first_step_seed: Write down the standards of the man you are *becoming*, not the one you *were*.
